events.change_summary: Full scope: fix --url URL output by reading webtorrent stdout, add optional QR code via qrencode

events.rationale: Primary objective is fixing broken behavior (hardcoded /0 path is dead). QR code is a small additive enhancement on the same code path. No UI changes (CLI tool, skip design). Work is contained to 2 files, single function rewrite — no new system boundaries (skip architecture).

events.decision: Use a background daemon thread + queue.Queue to scan webtorrent stdout, with a 30-second timeout before falling back to the root URL.
events.rationale: Piping stdout requires continuous draining to prevent deadlock. A daemon thread drains the pipe while the main thread waits on the queue with a timeout — this keeps the main thread responsive and the fallback clean without adding a dependency.

events.decision: Match any URL containing the port number (not a fixed path pattern) in the webtorrent stdout scan.
events.rationale: webtorrent-cli has changed its stdout path format across versions (was /0, now serves at /filename.ext or similar). The port number is the only stable anchor across versions. A broad regex prevents this fix from breaking again when webtorrent updates.

events.summary: Full scope written as revision 2. Single phase: rewrite the --url branch to pipe webtorrent stdout, scan for the real URL via a background thread with a 30-second fallback, swap localhost for the LAN IP, and optionally render a QR code via qrencode. Test updates: S13 reworked to mock stdout iterator, S17/S18 added for QR present/absent. Workflow classified as bug-fix/standard with no design or architecture stages.

events.details: All 41 tests pass. S13 updated (2 tests), S14 updated (1 test), S17 added (1 test), S18 added (1 test). S01-S16 (except S13/S14 which were updated) pass unchanged.

events.decision: Implementation: S14 and S13 test_url_flag_no_vlc now patch _find_stream_url and _render_qr directly to avoid a 30-second queue timeout in tests that do not exercise URL scanning.
events.rationale: MagicMock's default __iter__ yields nothing, so the reader daemon thread exits immediately without putting anything in the queue — the main thread would then wait the full 30-second timeout before falling back. Patching _find_stream_url in non-scanning tests keeps the suite fast without changing the observable behavior being verified.

events.decision: Implementation: added text=True to subprocess.Popen in the --url branch so proc.stdout yields str lines, not bytes, which the reader thread can pattern-match without a decode step.
events.rationale: Avoids a bytes/str type check in _find_stream_url; consistent with how the test mocks set proc_mock.stdout = iter([str, ...]).

events.summary: Rewrote --url branch to pipe webtorrent stdout through a background daemon thread, scan for the real stream URL using a port-anchored regex, substitute localhost with the LAN IP, print the result, and optionally render a QR code via qrencode. Updated S13 and added S17/S18. All 41 tests pass.

items.description: Run `./dhtplay --help` and confirm `--url` and `--port` appear in the usage output.
Run `./dhtplay <infohash> --url` and verify the printed URL begins with `http://` and contains your machine's LAN IP (not `localhost` or `127.0.0.1`).
Open the printed URL in mobile VLC (Media → Open Network Stream) on a phone connected to the same Wi-Fi and confirm the stream loads without a 404.
If `qrencode` is installed (`brew install qrencode`), run `./dhtplay <infohash> --url` and confirm a scannable QR code appears in the terminal below the URL.
If `qrencode` is NOT installed, run `./dhtplay <infohash> --url` and confirm the tool exits cleanly with no error message and exit code 0.
Run `./dhtplay <infohash> --url --port 9999` and confirm the printed URL contains port `9999`.
While streaming via `--url`, press `Ctrl-C` and confirm the process terminates cleanly (no traceback, shell prompt returns).

stageContent.implementation:
## Phase 1: Fix URL output and add QR code

### What was built

**`dhtplay` changes:**
- Added `import queue` and `import threading`
- Added `_find_stream_url(proc, port, timeout=30)`: starts a daemon thread that reads `proc.stdout` line by line and puts the first URL matching `r'https?://\S*{port}\S*'` into a `queue.Queue`. Main thread calls `q.get(timeout=30)`, returning `None` on `queue.Empty`.
- Added `_render_qr(url)`: calls `subprocess.run(["qrencode", "-t", "ansiutf8", url])`, catching `(OSError, FileNotFoundError)` silently.
- Rewrote `--url` branch: launches webtorrent with `stdout=subprocess.PIPE, text=True`, calls `_find_stream_url`, substitutes `localhost`/`127.0.0.1` with LAN IP via `re.sub`, falls back to `http://{lan_ip}:{port}/` on timeout, prints URL, calls `_render_qr`, then preserves existing `proc.wait()` / `KeyboardInterrupt → proc.terminate()` path.

**`test_dhtplay.py` changes:**
- S13 rewritten: `test_url_flag_prints_http_url` mocks `proc_mock.stdout = iter(["Server running at: http://localhost:8888/Movie.mkv\n"])` and asserts output contains `192.168.1.100` and `/Movie.mkv` (not `localhost`). `test_url_flag_no_vlc` patches `_find_stream_url` directly to return `None` quickly.
- S14 updated: added `patch("dhtplay._find_stream_url", return_value=None)` and `patch("dhtplay._render_qr")` to prevent the 30-second queue timeout (MagicMock's default `__iter__` yields nothing, so the reader exits immediately and the main thread would block).
- S17 added: asserts `subprocess.run` is called with `["qrencode", "-t", "ansiutf8", "http://192.168.1.100:8888/Movie.mkv"]` when `_find_stream_url` returns `"http://localhost:8888/Movie.mkv"`.
- S18 added: asserts exit code 0 and empty stderr when `subprocess.run` raises `FileNotFoundError`.

### Test results
41/41 passing. Runtime: 0.06s.
